natural.scenario: David and Rachel are watching their favorite hockey team play. As one of their team's players scores, they cheer.
natural.question: How likely is it that this goal is remembered for years to come?

natural.scenario: Joshua and Beverly, who are walking over to a friend's house downtown, come across a group of protesters. Beverly turns to Joshua.
natural.question: How likely is it that the protest requires riot police?

natural.scenario: William and Katherine are walking through an affluent neighborhood with many mansions. As they stop to admire one mansion in particular, William turns to Katherine.
natural.question: How likely is it that this mansion has more than 10 rooms?

natural.scenario: Amanda and Paul arrive at a restaurant that does not take reservations. Amanda glances around the dining area and turns to Paul.
natural.question: How likely are Amanda and Paul to wait long for a table?

natural.scenario: Lucy and Mark return home after a weekend getaway at a resort. As they unpack, Lucy smiles thinking about their accommodations.
natural.question: How likely is it that Lucy and Mark stayed at a five-star hotel?
natural.weak_utterance: The room we had was quite comfortable.
natural.strong_utterance: The room we had was quite luxurious.

natural.scenario: Linda and Kenneth are sitting around a campfire after a long day of hiking. As the stars begin to twinkle above them, Linda looks to Kenneth.
natural.question: How likely is it that Linda puts on an extra layer?
natural.weak_utterance: It's starting to get a bit cool.
natural.strong_utterance: It's starting to get a bit cold.

natural.scenario: Daniel and Brenda are having coffee at a trendy café. Brenda just met Daniel's girlfriend for the first time, and she looks pleasantly surprised as she turns to Daniel.
natural.question: How likely is it that Daniel's girlfriend often gets compliments about her looks?
natural.weak_utterance: You didn't tell me she was that cute.
natural.strong_utterance: You didn't tell me she was that beautiful.

natural.scenario: Bobby is meeting up with his friend Logan. When Bobby sees Logan, he notices that Logan has gotten a new haircut.
natural.question: How likely is Bobby to ask Logan where he got his haircut?

natural.scenario: Larry and Tina are driving through the countryside. As they pass the farmlands, Larry glances at the ground and turns to Tina.
natural.question: How likely is it that this part of the country is experiencing a drought?

natural.scenario: Carl and Laura are at a comedy club, enjoying a night of stand-up performances. At the end of the night, Carl thinks back, chuckles and turns to Laura.
natural.question: How likely is Carl to watch another performance by that last comedian?

natural.scenario: Jose and Jasmine have just attended a charity concert that included several musical performances. As they leave, Jose turns to Jasmine.
natural.question: How likely is it for Jose's ears to still be ringing from the concert?

natural.scenario: Marie and Jordan, two colleagues, are discussing a TED talk they just attended. Jordan asks Marie how she found the talk.
natural.question: How likely is it that Marie recommends watching the talk to others?

natural.scenario: Sharon is preparing dinner in the kitchen where her son Jason is trying to help. When Jason walks closer to the stove, Sharon turns to Jason.
natural.question: How likely is Jason to burn himself if he touches the pot?

natural.scenario: Jeremy and Marcus are attending a gaming convention. They're discussing a new game that was showcased during the event, particularly the visuals it offered. Jeremy turns to Marcus.
natural.question: How likely is the game to receive a five-star rating for its graphics?

natural.scenario: Ronald and Jess are sitting in a conference room brainstorming ideas for a new promotional campaign. After listening to Ronald's latest proposal, Jess turns to Ronald.
natural.question: How likely is it that Jess has never heard an idea like this before?

natural.scenario: Gerald and Margaret are attending a live musical concert. As the last song finishes, Gerald turns to Margaret.
natural.question: How likely is Gerald to give the musical performance a five-star rating?

natural.scenario: Timothy is visiting his doctor, Dr. Sanders, who is recommending that Timothy go through a minor operation. Dr. Sanders is describing the procedure and its risks.
natural.question: How likely is it that the operation will involve some anesthesia?

natural.scenario: Hannah and Jacob just finished a meal at a new restaurant in town. As they are getting ready to pay the bill, Hannah turns to Jacob.
natural.question: How likely is Hannah to leave a larger-than-usual tip?
natural.weak_utterance: I like how nice the staff is.
natural.strong_utterance: I like how polite the staff is.

natural.scenario: Catherine and Charles are visiting a small village during a road trip. After walking up a hill, they arrive at a viewpoint overlooking the entire village. Catherine turns to Charles.
natural.question: How likely is Catherine to take a photo of the view?

natural.scenario: Max and Judy are sitting in a library, studying for their final exams. Max turns to Judy.
natural.question: How likely is it that no talking is allowed in the library?
natural.weak_utterance: I like how quiet it is here.
natural.strong_utterance: I like how silent it is here.

natural.scenario: Megan and her husband Caleb are on a road trip together. As Megan drives, Caleb turns to her.
natural.question: How likely is it that Caleb asks Megan to get off at the next exit to eat?

natural.scenario: Tiffany and Matthew are academics at a university. Tiffany is expressing her opinion of Matthew's new research idea he just presented at a talk.
natural.question: How likely is it that no one has thought of this idea before?

natural.scenario: James and Lauren are looking to have a healthy snack in their kitchen. James picks up a banana, notices its texture, and turns to Lauren.
natural.question: How likely is it that the banana has gone bad?
natural.weak_utterance: This banana is kind of soft.
natural.strong_utterance: This banana is kind of mushy.

natural.scenario: Albert and Jessica are exploring a historic castle. They come across a wall whose size impresses them. Albert turns to Jessica.
natural.question: How likely is it that the wall could resist heavy cannon fire?

natural.scenario: Terry is telling Jenna about a movie he recently watched.
natural.question: How likely is it that Terry gives the movie a one-star rating?

natural.scenario: Jacqueline and Christian are observing a caterpillar which is resting on a leaf. They are interested in finding out how caterpillars move. Jacqueline turns to Christian.
natural.question: How likely is it for an average person to not notice the caterpillar's movement?

natural.scenario: Pamela and Bruce are exiting their classroom after having taken an exam. As they walk to their next class, Pamela turns to Bruce.
natural.question: How likely is it that Pamela failed the exam?

natural.scenario: Liam and Harper live together and are looking at the weather forecast for tomorrow. Harper looks to Liam.
natural.question: How likely is it that Harper and Liam will use their air conditioning tomorrow?
natural.weak_utterance: Looks like it's going to be warm tomorrow.
natural.strong_utterance: Looks like it's going to be hot tomorrow.

natural.scenario: Jack has just gone out to buy some milk during which it rained unexpectedly. When Jack returns home, Judith, Jack's partner, looks at him.
natural.question: How likely is Jack to change into dry clothes?

natural.scenario: Liam is discussing his daughter's piano recital performance with his colleague Sophie.
natural.question: How likely is it that Liam's daughter did not make any mistakes during the recital?
